title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) 2025: Program Overview and Study Areas
text: Computer Science BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 Computer science drives the fundamental technologies of today’s connected world, from healthcare to industrial applications, global trade, entertainment and security. Able to model and analyse challenges and provide solutions in every area of our lives, skilled computer scientists are highly sought after. Applications are welcomed for students who have followed the Arts, Commerce or Science streams. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. What you will study On this programme, you’ll find out about the latest advances in areas like machine learning, cyber security and computer vision. Through a mix of theoretical, design and practical subjects, you’ll learn about: basic computer science theory the ethical and legal frameworks which computer scientists and software engineers work within the practicalities of working with computers key techniques and application areas You’ll gain the knowledge to develop technologies that can make a difference to people’s lives in topics, such as: algorithmics computer systems data management artificial intelligence networks and security programming
summary: This chunk provides an overview of the BSc (Hons) Computer Science program, its duration, start date, and the fundamental areas students will study, emphasizing its equivalence to UK degrees.

title: Computer Science BSc (Hons): Detailed Course Modules and Electives
text: Course modules Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as options built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Find out why, when and how we might make changes. In Year 1, students will take 9 fixed course modules. In Year 2, students will take 7 fixed course modules. In Year 3, students will take 2 fixed course modules, and 5 optional electives. In Year 1, students must take all these course modules:- Algorithmics COMP1324 Computer Systems I COMP1313 COMP Part I Laboratory Programme COMP1300 Data Management COMP1314 Mathematics I COMP1311 Mathematics II COMP1321 Networks and Security COMP1323 Programming I COMP1312 Programming II COMP1322 In Year 2, students must take all these course modules:- Artificial Intelligence COMP2321 Computer Systems II COMP2323 Formal Specification and Verification COMP2313 Programming III COMP2312 Programming Language Concepts COMP2322 Software Design and Development Project COMP2300 Theory of Computing COMP2311 In Year 3, students must take all these course modules:- Part III Individual Project Phase 1 ECSP3000 Part III Individual Project Phase 2 ECSP3001 In Year 3, students must choose five of these optional electives:- Advanced Databases COMP3211 Cloud Application Development COMP3207 Computer Vision COMP3204 Game Design and Development COMP3218 Machine Learning Technologies COMP3222 Natural Language Processing COMP 3225 Security of Cyber Physical Systems COMP3217 Social Computing Techniques COMP3208 Web and Cloud Based Security COMP3226
summary: This chunk outlines the structure of the Computer Science BSc (Hons) program, detailing the fixed and optional modules students will undertake across the three academic years.

title: Computer Science Graduate Careers and University Employability Support
text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022). Graduating in computer science from a double-world top 100 university gives you a powerful advantage in the global job market. The digital sector is booming, with job creation outpacing other industries by double. Companies look to hire our graduates, confident they will make an immediate impact. Career opportunities With this Computer Science degree, you can pursue programming, software development, IT project management, or information systems oversight. Most of our graduates go straight into computer science jobs in high-tech companies. Potential roles include: software engineer app developer web developer systems analyst It’s also a great foundation for further study at master’s degree or PhD level. Companies you could work at University of Southampton computer science graduates have secured roles at organisations including: Apple Sony Cisco Systems Audi Microsoft Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi As well as support from our team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals through our MyCareer platform Through our e-mentoring scheme, you’ll also have access to: tailored career planning advice on employability skills industry insight We have incorporated our employability skills model, Successful Futures, into our curriculum. Our links with multi-national and industrial organisations mean that you can expect to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. You will receive a personalised employability programme that will provide excellent prospects for securing a premium placement and financial security on graduating. We also host regular alumni events hosted in person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
summary: This chunk highlights career opportunities for Computer Science graduates, potential roles, companies that hire them, and the comprehensive employability support offered by University of Southampton Delhi.

title: Computer Science BSc (Hons): Entry Requirements and Language Proficiency
text: Key information To help plan your year we have published University of Southampton Delhi term and holiday dates in advance, but these may be subject to change. We expect to provide 25 seats on this programme for academic year 25/26. Eligibility criteria For Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 82% in your India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and a minimum of 85% in Mathematics (Standard X) examination For Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE): Minimum 82% in your Indian School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 85% in Mathematics (Standard X) examination For State Board: Minimum 87% in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination (overall) and Minimum 85% in Mathematics in Mathematics (Standard X) examination International Baccalaureate: 38 points and above, including 19 points at Higher level, including 6 at Higher Level Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches) or 7 at Higher Level (Applications and Interpretations) A levels: A*AA including Grade A and above in Mathematics English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
summary: This chunk details the academic eligibility criteria for the Computer Science BSc (Hons) program for various Indian boards and international qualifications, alongside the English language proficiency requirements.
entities: Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE)

title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) 2025: Tuition Fees and Financial Aid
text: Our fees You can pay your tuition fee in up to 4 instalments. The fees for a year’s study, starting in August 2025, are: 13,20,000 INR for India students 15,40,000 INR for Overseas students Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit within 28 days of receiving a conditional offer, and no later than 14 days before the start of term. For students starting in August 2025, the deposit is: 132,000 INR for India students 154,000 INR for Overseas students You’ll then pay the balance of your yearly tuition fee as you study, in up to 3 instalments. If you’re an India student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 316,800 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 435,600 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 435,600 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 If you’re an Overseas student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 369,600 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 508,200 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 508,200 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar. Funding Scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to academically gifted students and talented students who would benefit from study financially through a merit bursary. Funding support Please email us about the partnerships we have developed with trusted and reputable financial institutions keen to support University of Southampton Delhi students with a range of education loans.
summary: This chunk outlines the tuition fees for the Computer Science BSc (Hons) program for India and Overseas students, including instalment plans, deposit requirements, scholarships, and education loan partnerships.

title: Computer Science BSc (Hons): How to Apply and Admissions FAQs
text: Our application process Admissions are now open for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR. Our first cohort for undergraduate bachelor’s degrees starts in August 2025. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. Download more details This downloadable document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, the programme structure, information on course modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. PDF BSc Hons Computer Science 2025 Programme Description Document 89 KB How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year. Apply now You might also want to know Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available.
summary: This chunk explains the application process for the Computer Science BSc (Hons) program, including guidance for applicants, downloadable program details, and FAQs regarding scholarships and predicted scores.
entities: BSc Hons Computer Science 2025 Programme Description Document

title: Honours Degree Benefits and Computer Science Programme Structure FAQs
text: Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
summary: This chunk addresses frequently asked questions regarding the benefits of an Honours degree, its three-year duration, the distinction between BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) awards, and the role of optional modules.
